answer:
**Manusmṛti chapter 11 in this corpus covers rules for charitable gifts (dāna) to specific categories of Brahmin snātakas (e.g., family-establishers, travelers, students, the sick) up to verse 11.6.**[^1]

No passages in the available text of chapter 11—or elsewhere in the Manusmṛti translation—discuss *prayascittas* (penances or expiations) for drinking *surā* (intoxicants). Searches for terms like "surā," "sura," "intoxicant," "liquor," "alcohol," "drinking sura," and combinations with "penance," "expiation," or "prayascitta" yielded no relevant matches in this work.[^2]

**The chapter text appears incomplete** (only 6 paragraphs / ~206 words / verses 11.1–6), lacking the expected full content on penances (typically verses ~11.7–266 in standard editions, including *surāpāna* prohibitions and atonements around 11.86–94). The corpus may not include the full chapter, so this specific topic cannot be located or cited here.

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, chapter 11 (translation), [chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/11/translation/).
[^2]: Corpus-wide searches returned unrelated hits (e.g., Vishnu Smṛti on purification from intoxicants; Yama Smṛti 1.14 explicitly on *surā* penance—but nothing in Manusmṛti).
